name: Trafilatura
tagline: Crawl the web and pull clean main text and metadata out of messy HTML
seoTitle: Trafilatura: Web Text & Metadata Extraction in Python
metaDescription: Trafilatura is a Python package and CLI that crawls pages and extracts the main text, metadata, and comments while stripping ads and boilerplate.
overview: Trafilatura is a Python package and command-line tool for gathering text on the web and turning raw HTML into structured data. It handles the full path from discovery to output: crawling, downloading, scraping, and extracting the main text, metadata, and comments from a page.

It is built for developers and researchers who need clean article content without the surrounding noise. By focusing on the actual content and dropping recurring elements like headers and footers, it produces text that is easier to feed into search indexes, datasets, or NLP pipelines. No database is required, and the output converts to common formats.

Within web scraping and crawling tools, Trafilatura sits at the extraction layer. It supports sitemaps and feeds for finding URLs, processes both live and previously downloaded HTML, and can emit TXT, Markdown, CSV, JSON, HTML, XML, and XML-TEI.
features: Extracts main text plus metadata such as title, author, date, site name, categories, and tags
Discovers content through sitemaps (TXT, XML) and feeds (ATOM, JSON, RSS) with URL filtering and deduplication
Processes both live URLs and previously downloaded HTML files or parsed trees, with parallel download queues
Preserves structure: paragraphs, titles, lists, quotes, code, line breaks, and inline formatting, plus optional comments, links, images, and tables
Outputs TXT, Markdown, CSV, JSON, HTML, XML, and XML-TEI
Optional add-ons for language detection on extracted content and speed optimizations

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, then extract main text from a URL using either the command line or the Python API.

steps.heading: Install Trafilatura
steps.body: Install the package from PyPI with pip.

steps.heading: Extract from the command line
steps.body: Pass a single URL with the -u flag to print its main text.

steps.heading: Extract with Python
steps.body: Use fetch_url to download a page and extract to get its main text.
steps.code:
from trafilatura import fetch_url, extract

downloaded = fetch_url('https://github.blog/2019-03-29-leader-spotlight-erin-spiceland/')
result = extract(downloaded)
print(result)

useCases: Building text datasets from many web pages for research or NLP and LLM training
Cleaning article content for search indexing or a reading or archiving pipeline
Crawling a site through its sitemap or feed and exporting articles to JSON, CSV, or Markdown
Pulling page metadata like author, date, and title alongside the body text
